title: Bill Hart (infielder)

infobox.value: (March 4, 1913 – July 29, 1968) was an American [third baseman](/wiki/Third_baseman "Third baseman") and [shortstop](/wiki/Shortstop "Shortstop") in [Major League Baseball](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball "Major League Baseball") who played from 1943 to 1945 for the [Brooklyn Dodgers](/wiki/Brooklyn_Dodgers "Brooklyn Dodgers").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Born in [Wiconisco, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Wiconisco,_Pennsylvania "Wiconisco, Pennsylvania"), he died at age 55 in [Lykens, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Lykens,_Pennsylvania "Lykens, Pennsylvania").
["Bill Hart"](https://www.baseball-reference.com/players/h/hartbi03.shtml). *Baseball Reference*. Sports Reference LLC. Retrieved June 27, 2022.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American baseball player (1913-1968)

For the baseball pitcher of the same name, see [Bill Hart (pitcher)](/wiki/Bill_Hart_(pitcher) "Bill Hart (pitcher)").

**William Woodrow Hart** (March 4, 1913 – July 29, 1968) was an American [third baseman](/wiki/Third_baseman "Third baseman") and [shortstop](/wiki/Shortstop "Shortstop") in [Major League Baseball](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball "Major League Baseball") who played from 1943 to 1945 for the [Brooklyn Dodgers](/wiki/Brooklyn_Dodgers "Brooklyn Dodgers").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Born in [Wiconisco, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Wiconisco,_Pennsylvania "Wiconisco, Pennsylvania"), he died at age 55 in [Lykens, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Lykens,_Pennsylvania "Lykens, Pennsylvania").
